comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Verified in current checkout as an architectural consistency issue. Tool metadata is generally provided via TypeBox, but runtime param decoding is split across multiple systems: agent/tool.ts, memory/index.ts, skill-manage/index.ts, and exa/index.ts define TypeBox parameter metadata plus separate Effect Schema decoders via Schema.decodeUnknownSync(...); thread/index.ts instead uses TypeBox metadata and runtime decoding via Value.Parse(...) from @sinclair/typebox/value. So there is a real split-brain boundary today: two different runtime decoder systems are in active use for tool params, while TypeBox remains the SDK-facing metadata source. This is not an immediate bug if schemas stay aligned, but it does create schema drift risk and inconsistent error behavior.
